title: Episode 1 – The Adventure Begins
logline: Keen leaves home wearing his father’s empty cuffs, loses his way in a storm, and discovers that courage is love carried into frightening water before magic ever names it.
declaration: This episode is about the first brave step away from home: the ache of leaving someone who loves you, and the discovery that love can travel with you into frightening water. When it ends, the child should feel, “I can be scared and still move”; the parent beside them should feel the sting and pride of letting a child become bigger without becoming unreachable. The scene that carries the most weight is Scene 7, out at sea and underwater, because Keen stops performing bravery and simply chooses someone else while the storm is still real. The colour of this episode is storm-washed aquamarine — cold fear lit from inside by a small, faithful glow. The moment I am most proud of is Keen seeing his father’s wristbands begin to glow before any crystal is awarded — because it trusts the child to feel something real: courage was already in him, carried by love, before the magic named it.

theme.declaration: This episode is about the first brave step away from home: the ache of leaving someone who loves you, and the discovery that love can travel with you into frightening water. When it ends, the child should feel, “I can be scared and still move”; the parent beside them should feel the sting and pride of letting a child become bigger without becoming unreachable. The scene that carries the most weight is Scene 7, out at sea and underwater, because Keen stops performing bravery and simply chooses someone else while the storm is still real. The colour of this episode is storm-washed aquamarine — cold fear lit from inside by a small, faithful glow. The moment I am most proud of is Keen seeing his father’s wristbands begin to glow before any crystal is awarded — because it trusts the child to feel something real: courage was already in him, carried by love, before the magic named it.
theme.theme: True courage is not feeling fearless; it is carrying love with you into the unknown and choosing the next right thing while you are still scared.
theme.leadArc: Keen wants to prove he is ready, capable, and brave enough to leave home on his own; underneath, he needs to learn that bravery is not a performance of certainty but a choice made through fear, supported by the love he already carries and the new belonging he allows himself to receive.
theme.storySpine: Once upon a time, in Crystal Cove and beyond, there was a young bear named Keen who wanted to begin his own adventure. Every day, he tried to look ready and brave, checking everything twice so no one would see the wobble underneath. Until one day, he left his Mum with his father’s wristbands on his paws and sailed into water bigger than he expected. Because of that, a storm swallowed his map, his boat, and his certainty. Because of that, when Squeaky was trapped, Keen stopped trying to look brave and acted from the love and courage already inside him. Until finally, the Crystal Bears witnessed what he had chosen, and the Aquamarine crystal settled into the wristbands as recognition, not rescue. And ever since then, Keen knows that growing up does not mean going alone — it means carrying home with you and letting a new family find you too.
theme.promise: By the final frame, the audience must feel warm, windblown, and safe after danger — like they have survived a storm, made a friend, and discovered that being scared did not disqualify them from being brave.
theme.throughline: The episode must keep joy and ache in the same hand: Fuzzby’s pollen chaos and honey friendship make the world delightful, but underneath are Keen’s mother letting go, his absent father present through the wristbands, a lost boat, and the quiet cost of becoming bigger; the ending is homecoming, not a reset.
theme.selCompetency: Courage — Keen’s Aquamarine arc teaches responsible decision-making through embodied action: he notices fear, chooses care over panic, and discovers that brave choices can be made without feeling fully ready.
theme.haidt: For the parent, the episode speaks to the anxious-generation fear of letting children take real risks, separate from constant protection, and navigate uncertainty; it argues gently that resilience grows through supported independence, embodied adventure, and being trusted to act, not through a world made perfectly safe.
theme.pressureTest: This is true, universal, and earnable if every scene keeps courage rooted in fear, love, and action rather than spectacle. The trap is making Keen look like a fearless action hero, making the Crystal Call feel like a power-up, or letting Aida explain the lesson too neatly. The episode must avoid saccharine certainty: Mum’s goodbye should hurt, the storm should feel genuinely too big, the wristbands must glow as inner truth rather than magic equipment, and the final family feeling must not erase the ache of leaving home or the danger Keen passed through.

beatRule: one 10-12s Seedance take per beat — the beat directs its own internal cuts (director skill v5.0)
_note: Authored by cb_director (Gate 1, BEAT-NATIVE) from the script — 45 beats, 10 scenes.
style: Polished 3D CGI animation — modern feature-film computer-generated imagery (Pixar/DreamWorks quality): fully 3D-modelled characters and environments, physically-based rendering, soft global illumination and volumetric lighting, subsurface scattering on plush fur, large expressive eyes with warm catch-lights, realistic materials, cinematic depth of field. 16:9. NOT 2D, NOT hand-drawn, NOT flat.

scenes.name: Rainforest Pollen Run
scenes.location: A sunlit wildflower meadow in Crystal Cove, framed at bee-flight height so ordinary lavender spikes, daisies and clover loom like a towering blossom corridor. Sparkling heart-shaped light trails and floating cut crystals (quartz, amethyst, opal, garnet) drift among the stems and rest low in the grass; warm rolling hills glow gold-pink in the deep background.
scenes.weather: Warm breeze under mostly clear sky; distant pressure beginning to build but not yet visible.
scenes.lighting: Warm golden-pink backlight glows low across the meadow, haloing the lavender and daisy tops with soft god-rays and floating sparkle; first faint coolness creeps into the far hills by the thunder beat.
scenes.look: Empty establishing plate filmed low, at bee-flight height, looking ACROSS the meadow so ordinary blooms loom tall and frame-filling — foreground, midground and background all thick with purple lavender spikes, white daisies and pink-red clover, with a clear low flight corridor winding between the stems for rapid aerial zig-zags, dips into flowers, leaf brushes and blossom crashes. Sparkling heart-shaped light trails loop through the middle distance; clusters of floating cut crystals in many colours (clear quartz, amethyst, opal, garnet, citrine) hover among the blooms and rest low in the grass, with two soft glowing pink orbs drifting centrally. Warm golden-pink light backlights the whole frame, haloing the flowers; soft rolling hills recede into a warm gold-pink haze in the deep background. No storm yet, no characters.
scenes.emotionalCore: This scene is really about joy before the world turns: the audience must feel safe enough to laugh, then feel the first tiny prickle that adventure will ask for courage.
scenes.sceneShotName: Rainforest Pollen Run — sunlit wildflower meadow, bee's-eye low
scenes.definingFeature: a ground-level wildflower corridor — lavender, daisies and clover that loom tall at bee-flight height — laced with floating hearts and cut crystals
scenes.colorTemperature: warm golden-amber morning safety with a faint cool sky undertone
scenes.cameraHeight: low bee's-eye height, just above the grass, looking across the meadow so ordinary blooms loom tall

scenes.name: Aida’s Sanctuary Vision
scenes.location: A quiet sheltered cove sanctuary at the edge of Crystal Cove, where gentle waves meet a smooth shore and a forest path begins just beyond the crystal bowl.
scenes.weather: Clear but the distant wind is beginning to gather beyond the sanctuary.
scenes.lighting: Soft rose-gold sanctuary light, warm and still, with Aida’s vision glow becoming the emotional source before fading into wind-cooled edges.
scenes.look: Empty sanctuary plate with the crystal bowl and wand resting beside it dominating the foreground-left at the shore’s edge; gentle wavelets roll in from screen-right across bright blue water, while the forest entrance sits midground-left for Aida’s later exit. The frame is still, protective, and uncluttered, with soft late-morning canopy light, faint rose-quartz glow around the bowl area, and distant treetops subtly stirred by gathering wind. No characters.
scenes.emotionalCore: This scene is really about a mentor sensing a child’s first step away from home; the audience must feel that love is already reaching toward Keen before the storm does.
scenes.sceneShotName: Aida’s sanctuary shore — late-morning rose-gold wide with crystal bowl
scenes.definingFeature: the crystal singing bowl set at the sanctuary shoreline, catching rose-gold light as waves quiet around it
scenes.colorTemperature: warm late-morning amber softened by rose-quartz tenderness, with the first cool hint of distant wind
scenes.cameraHeight: grounded eye-level from the shore, slightly low toward the bowl

scenes.name: The Pier Goodbye
scenes.location: The Crystal Cove pier at the edge of the beach: a weathered wooden dock projecting into clear open sea, with enough water beside it for Squeaky to surface and enough horizon beyond for Keen’s boat to leave screen-right.
scenes.time: Late morning, continuous emotional flow from Aida’s vision
scenes.weather: Clear and breezy, with the first subtle signs of unsettled air over the open sea.
scenes.lighting: Warm golden morning sunlight on wood and water, gentle sparkle on waves, with a faint cooler line on the far horizon.
scenes.look: An empty, emotionally open stage dominated by the dock and the small sailboat moored alongside it: dock planks run from foreground-left toward midframe, the loaded departure area sits near the cleat with a rolled map, satchel, berry pouch and blanket neatly ready, the sailboat bobs screen-right on a tied rope, and wide sparkling water stretches beyond with faint unsettled ripples under a breezy late-morning sky. The composition leaves clear space on the dock for the goodbye, clear water beside the boat for a sudden splash, and a long open horizon for the boat to drift away; no characters present.
scenes.emotionalCore: This scene is really about the ache of being trusted to go; the audience must feel that Keen’s bravery begins with a goodbye that hurts.
scenes.sceneShotName: Crystal Cove pier — late-morning goodbye, moored sailboat wide
scenes.definingFeature: the small moored sailboat tied to the goodbye pier, waiting between safe dock and wide open water
scenes.colorTemperature: warm late-morning amber safety with cool aquamarine sea-fill, love beginning to feel like distance
scenes.cameraHeight: low child-height on the dock, looking gently out toward the boat and open water

scenes.name: Lost at Sea
scenes.location: Endless open water beyond Crystal Cove, with no land in sight: a small sailboat space on roughening waves, open horizon, and enough surrounding water for Squeaky to surface, vanish, leap, and signal beside the boat.
scenes.weather: Clear sea turning rapidly to heavy storm: gusts, dark clouds, hard rain, lightning far off, roughening waves.
scenes.lighting: Gold drains into blue-grey storm light; rain sheets flatten the horizon until only cold flashes and wet reflections remain.
scenes.look: An empty, cinematic ocean plate dominated by vast water and a fast-arriving storm wall. The small sailboat sits low in frame-left/midground, bow angled screen-right with mast and sail up, surrounded by choppy waves and sloshing deck space; the path ahead opens into frame-right where the sea darkens. The horizon is wide but being erased by grey rain sheets, with distant lightning buried in the cloud bank. No characters; no land; no extra vessels; only the boat, open sea, rough water, rain, wind-torn sail, storm clouds, and erased horizon the scene requires.
scenes.emotionalCore: This scene is really about the moment performing readiness fails; the audience must feel the stomach-drop of being lost and the effort of choosing one steady breath anyway.
scenes.sceneShotName: Open sea — storm front swallowing small sailboat, high wide
scenes.definingFeature: the tiny sailboat being swallowed by an enormous grey storm horizon
scenes.colorTemperature: cool blue-silver storm fear, faintly storm-washed aquamarine from the sea
scenes.cameraHeight: slightly high looking down, making the boat feel small in the vast water

scenes.name: Rainforest Edge Alarm
scenes.location: The outer edge of the rainforest where a narrow bee-height flight corridor breaks through wind-bent trees toward the beach and stormy open water beyond.
scenes.time: Early afternoon, continuous with the storm
scenes.weather: Storm building hard: strong wind, rain beginning to cut through the trees, branches swaying sharply.
scenes.lighting: Cool blue-grey storm light under the canopy, with occasional pale sea-glare beyond the trees.
scenes.look: An empty rain-lashed rainforest edge staged left-to-right: dense wet leaves and swaying branches frame a clear flight path from frame-left toward a beach-facing gap on frame-right; a low branch cuts across the right-side flight path for the imminent bonk; through the shifting rain beyond the trees, the grey sea is visible with a tiny boat far in the distance rising and falling between dark waves. The frame is dominated by whipping foliage, diagonal rain, and the dangerous sightline to the water; no characters, no extra props.
scenes.emotionalCore: This scene is really about silliness becoming care in an instant; the audience must feel Fuzzby’s chaos snap into purpose when he sees someone else in danger.
scenes.sceneShotName: Rainforest edge — storm-lashed sea gap, bee's-eye wide
scenes.definingFeature: a storm-dark break in the rainforest that frames the tiny boat beyond the rain
scenes.colorTemperature: cool blue-grey storm fear with drained green-gold forest undertone
scenes.cameraHeight: bee-flight height, slightly low looking through the corridor toward the sea

scenes.name: Crystal Cove Beacon
scenes.location: The wet Crystal Cove shoreline at the water’s edge, with rain-dark sea stretching screen-right into the storm and a shallow strip of beach wide enough for the bears to gather in a protective circle.
scenes.weather: Storm building over the cove: heavy clouds, wind through grasses, rain beginning to blur the sea.
scenes.lighting: Cold storm-blue environment interrupted by warm crystal pulses; the combined beacon becomes the eye-leading light across the water.
scenes.look: Empty storm-shore plate: slick wet sand and waterline dominate the lower frame, with the stormy sea opening screen-right toward the horizon; wind-bent grasses frame the landward side, and the necessary beach clutter — one shell, one piece of driftwood, and small fixed shore debris — sits in the foreground/midground for Fuzzby’s crash path. Heavy clouds press low, rain begins to blur the distance, and the composition leaves a clear central gathering space near the water’s edge where the beacon can form and shoot across the dark water.
scenes.emotionalCore: This scene is really about belonging reaching outward before it has even met Keen; the audience must feel that help is not control, it is a light held for someone in the dark.
scenes.sceneShotName: Crystal Cove shore — storm-building beacon beach, wide waterline plate
scenes.definingFeature: the open storm-dark sea corridor beyond the wet waterline, aimed like a path for a crystal beacon
scenes.colorTemperature: cool blue-grey storm fear with faint aquamarine crystal promise
scenes.cameraHeight: low shore-level, slightly looking out across the water

scenes.name: Underwater Rescue
scenes.location: Off the rain-lashed Crystal Cove shore where the storm sea drops into a blue-grey underwater rescue channel, with the water’s edge on screen-left and open churning water driving screen-right.
scenes.time: Early afternoon, the darkest point of the storm
scenes.weather: Full storm: crashing waves above, rain-lashed surface, violent current below, foam and lightning-muted water.
scenes.lighting: Darkest palette of the episode: cold underwater blue-grey, storm flashes above, and a faint aquamarine glow from Keen’s empty cuffs becoming the emotional light.
scenes.look: Empty split-level establishing plate: the frame is dominated by towering storm waves and foam above the waterline, with the rain-battered shore and aquamarine-white crystal beacon glowing through haze at screen-left; below the surface, muted blue-grey current reveals a submerged rock ledge and a ragged drift net twisting in the pull of the water. A small storm-tossed sailboat sits far out on the surface as the necessary launch point for the dive. No characters. Lightning-muted cloud cover, rain streaks, suspended bubbles, and violent current make the world feel too big while the beacon gives one thin line of hope.
scenes.emotionalCore: This scene is really about the difference between looking brave and choosing bravely; the audience must feel Keen act through fear because someone needs him.
scenes.sceneShotName: Crystal Cove storm sea — split-level rescue wide, shore beacon and submerged rock
scenes.definingFeature: the storm-lit split waterline revealing both the crashing surface and the submerged rock-and-drift-net rescue zone beneath it
scenes.colorTemperature: cool blue-silver fear with a faint aquamarine-white beacon glow
scenes.cameraHeight: low split-level at the waterline, slightly canted by the swell, looking from shore-left toward the open storm sea

scenes.name: Welcome to Crystal Cove
scenes.location: The sheltered Crystal Cove beach at the edge of the shallows, where wet sand meets softening waves beneath a crystal-veined cove wall.
scenes.time: Mid afternoon, immediately after the rescue
scenes.weather: Rain easing; storm breaking apart; waves still active but softening near shore.
scenes.lighting: Cool storm light begins warming at the edges; wet sand reflects returning gold and soft crystal glow.
scenes.look: Empty wide plate with the wet shoreline dominating the foreground and midground: a clear central patch of glistening sand for Keen’s landing and the gathered welcome circle, shallow water and gentle active waves behind it for Squeaky’s glide, leap and dive, and the curved cove shelter framing the background with small glowing crystals catching the breaking storm light. Rain has nearly stopped; puddles, sea-sheen and softened surf keep the danger present while warm space opens on the beach. No characters, no extra props.
scenes.emotionalCore: This scene is really about being received after danger; the audience must feel Keen’s shaky relief turn into the first warmth of a new family.
scenes.sceneShotName: Crystal Cove shore — rain-easing welcome circle wide
scenes.definingFeature: the crystal-veined cove wall curving protectively around the wet welcome shore
scenes.colorTemperature: soft warm aquamarine after-storm — safety returning through rain
scenes.cameraHeight: low bear-eye shoreline height, looking slightly across the wet sand toward the shallows

scenes.name: Aquamarine Welcome Circle
scenes.location: A sheltered Crystal Cove gathering circle just beyond the beach, with a broad open center, damp crystal-veined ground, and rainforest leaves enclosing the space after the storm has cleared.
scenes.time: Late afternoon, after the storm has passed
scenes.weather: Storm cleared; damp air, glistening leaves, calm breeze returning.
scenes.lighting: Hopeful warm light returns through cool post-rain air; aquamarine shimmer becomes the ceremonial emotional source, then settles warm.
scenes.look: Empty ceremonial wide plate: the open circular center dominates the frame, large enough for the full bear circle, the aquamarine awarding, and the later group hug. Aida’s side is naturally staged screen-left of center and Keen’s side screen-right of center, with a clear taller back arc and nearer front curve for the gathered friends. Rain droplets cling to leaves and low crystal stones; the ground glistens softly, washed clean, with calm air and late-afternoon sunlight returning through the canopy. No characters, no extra props, nothing competing with the center where the aquamarine light will appear.
scenes.emotionalCore: This scene is really about courage being witnessed rather than granted; the audience must feel the crystal recognize what Keen already carried.
scenes.sceneShotName: Crystal Cove welcome circle — storm-washed late-afternoon wide
scenes.definingFeature: a rain-washed circular welcome space ringed by low aquamarine crystal-veined stones
scenes.colorTemperature: storm-cleared warm gold with cool aquamarine glints — fear witnessed into belonging
scenes.cameraHeight: gentle eye-level, slightly low enough to make the circle feel embracing

scenes.name: Honey Friendship Walk
scenes.location: Crystal Cove’s damp golden-sand shoreline where the calm surf meets the crystal-veined cove edge, with the beach stretching clearly screen-right for a group walk.
scenes.time: Early evening golden hour, continuous emotional release after the welcome circle
scenes.weather: Clear after storm; peaceful breeze, settled surf, rain still clinging to leaves and sand.
scenes.lighting: Warm golden-hour light, brighter and more settled than the start, with soft aquamarine traces in the environment and wet surfaces holding the memory of the storm.
scenes.look: A wide empty beach plate built for a warm screen-right walking exit: wet reflective sand dominates the foreground and midground, gentle waves lap along frame-left, and the shoreline curves into the bright peaceful cove in the background. Rain droplets still cling to nearby leaves at the upper edge and sparkle on the sand, keeping the storm’s memory visible. The frame stays open and horizontal, with enough clear sand path for the full group to move together; no characters, no extra props.
scenes.emotionalCore: This scene is really about the first ordinary joke after a life-changing storm; the audience must feel that belonging can be funny, hungry, and still carry the ache of what was left behind.
scenes.sceneShotName: Crystal Cove beach — post-storm golden-hour shoreline, wide walking plate
scenes.definingFeature: the crystal-veined cove wall glowing softly over rain-polished golden sand
scenes.colorTemperature: warm amber golden-hour safety with faint storm-washed aquamarine coolness in the wet sand
scenes.cameraHeight: bear eye-level, slightly low and open toward the shoreline

beats.storyBeat: The giant flower field is safe, warm and playful as Fuzzby and Zenny collect pollen; Zenny works with calm precision while Fuzzby turns the job into a manic aerial performance, overdoes a dip into a flower, ricochets off a leaf, and proudly buttons the crash.
beats.emotionalIntent: The audience should feel instant buoyant safety: a sunny little belly-laugh before any danger exists, with Fuzzby's pride making the bonk lovable rather than cruel.
beats.want: Fuzzby wants to look like a brilliant, official pollen professional while Zenny wants to complete the work neatly.
beats.need: Fuzzby needs to be loved as his messy, enthusiastic self; Zenny needs to let affection show without surrendering her dry precision.
beats.crystalTruth: No bear crystal is present in this scene; the truth is carried by the environment instead: warm pollen light stays steady around Zenny's calm competence while it scatters wildly around Fuzzby's performed competence, revealing the need beneath his proud face.
beats.kidRead: Fuzzby zooms around singing, crashes into a leaf, and acts like he meant it.
beats.adultRead: A child trying to look capable is most endearing when the performance cracks and they recover with dignity.
beats.theGame: Pollen Run Rules: collect pollen like a serious professional, but every flower secretly tests whether Fuzzby can survive his own confidence.
beats.physicalFeeling: A fizzy upward bounce in the chest that lands as a soft FWIP in the ribs within the first two seconds.
beats.light: Warm golden morning god-rays isolate the flower corridor; pollen motes sparkle around Fuzzby's path while Zenny remains in cleaner, calmer amber light.
beats.atmosphere: Floating pollen, swaying petals, and soft breeze make the air feel edible and safe; leaves at bee-height create playful obstacle rhythm.
beats.motionTempo: Fuzzby is full-throttle hyper from frame one, zig-zagging, bumping and snapping back; Zenny works calm, precise, neat and efficient in the SAME frame, never cutting away to alternate coverage.
beats.grade: Saturated honey-amber with gentle green-teal forest fill, preserving warmth after the FWIP so the bonk never feels mean.

cuts.framing: Drone-style chase opens low and wide through towering flowers, gentle organic push
cuts.action: Tall flowers sway gently, everything beautiful, as Fuzzby and Zenny weave flower to flower collecting pollen together; Zenny's path is smooth and precise while Fuzzby zig-zags wildly and loudly hums as he works, wings beating rapidly and continuously while airborne.
cuts.delivery: fun sing-song rhythm; puffed-up pride, breathless tempo, delighted with himself before the gag lands

cuts.framing: The camera picks Fuzzby up and CHASES him drone-style — high, low, round and round — as he builds speed
cuts.action: Fuzzby zigzags wilder, humming louder and louder, dips low into a flower and scoops up pollen, overdoes the exit, spins sideways into a leaf with a soft FWIP, and bounces back into the air; wings keep flapping fast with motion blur.

cuts.framing: Camera settles close on Fuzzby for the button as Zenny glides into the same frame
cuts.action: As if nobody has seen him, Fuzzby instantly straightens into a wicked superhero chest-out pose; Zenny glides up beside him and watches for the beat, still working neatly.
cuts.delivery: crisp, dignified cover-up; a tiny beat of delayed self-certainty after the FWIP

beats.cameraArc: Drone-style camera chases Fuzzby the whole beat — high, low, round and round — building speed with him through the weave, the dive and the FWIP; it never sits wide and observes. The ONLY hold in the whole beat is close on Fuzzby's own proud chest-out pose as Zenny glides into frame beside him, under 1.5 seconds. No separate reaction shot, no locked two-shot ending.
beats.pauseHold: After 'Nailed it.' hold Fuzzby's own proud chest-out pose, with Zenny arriving to watch in the same frame, for under 1.5 seconds — the beat's only hold; music buttons the moment.

performance.surface: Fuzzby performs expert competence; Zenny performs calm efficiency.
performance.underneath: Fuzzby is desperate to be impressive and accepted; Zenny is already amused and fond, though she refuses to give him the satisfaction yet.
performance.innerThought: Fuzzby: I am doing this better than anyone has ever done pollen. Zenny: This is going to be a long morning.

beats.crystalGlow: No character crystal glow in this beat; environmental pollen glow steady-warm, with Fuzzby's pollen trail scattering chaotically and Zenny's staying clean and even.
beats.startState: Opening frame: Fuzzby is frame-left mid-hover at bee height between tall pollen-filled blossoms, already poised in a proud working posture; Zenny is frame-right among neighboring blossoms in a calm collecting pose; both are airborne with wings in rapid continuous flap; flowers, grass corridors, pollen motes and 2-3 soft ambient crystals fill the rainforest flower-field plate.
beats.soundIntent: Warm morning insects, soft petal rustle, fast continuous bee wings; Fuzzby's sing-song rides a light pizzicato motif that chases him, FWIP lands with a soft leafy snap and tiny percussion puff, then a small proud button chord under the line in @Audio1 as the music ducks.

continuity.opensFrom: Scene opens from black into the warm deep rainforest flower field with both bees already present and working.
continuity.carryToNext: Fuzzby remains airborne frame-left, proud and overconfident; Zenny frame-right has just glided up to watch and is close enough to be the counterweight for the next flower gag.

check.focalSubject: Fuzzby, with Zenny as the deadpan counter-frame.
check.emotionalRead: Safe, fizzy delight with a first clear read of the Fuzzby/Zenny comic engine.
check.heartCheck: The child laughs at the FWIP and the proud cover-up; the parent recognizes the tender little performance of competence underneath the chaos.

beats.shot_style: Pixar-style warm organic rainforest comedy; wide-to-close gag clock with locked deadpan topper.
beats.gag_carry: Fuzzby’s bravado survives the first bonk and escalates into wanting to look official.
beats.director_mode: Comedy-first, BIG, but never cruel; Fuzzby is the lovable engine and Zenny’s stillness is the frame.
beats.audience_feeling_target: A sunny belly-laugh that says this world is safe enough to be silly in.
beats.emotional_function: Scene establish, hook, first action gag, first reaction hold.
beats.script_truth_lock: Use script action order exactly: pollen work, BIZZY line, flower dip, leaf FWIP, proud recovery, Nailed it, Zenny glides up and watches.
beats.music_emotion: Playful pizzicato warmth with tiny pride flourish.
beats.performance_notes: Fuzzby’s pride is chest-forward superhero/on-parade, not body inflation; Zenny’s comedy is minimal: a slow look, a blink, no big gesture.
beats.physical_staging_intent: Clear left-right two-hander: Fuzzby’s chaotic path crosses his own lane while Zenny’s precise lane stays clean.
beats.visibility_rule: Both bees visible in the opening frame; keep Fuzzby frame-left and Zenny frame-right whenever together.
beats.contact_rule: Leaf impact is a soft FWIP contact with visible squash/overshoot/settle; no injury, no meanness.
beats.physics_rule: Airborne bees must always have rapidly flapping wings; Fuzzby’s rebound has mass and timing, never floaty.
beats.visual_payoff_rule: The beautiful opening flower-field warmth must remain legible behind the gag.
beats.failed_correction_rule: If the model makes Fuzzby graceful or slow, reroll with full-throttle chaotic zig-zag and weighted FWIP emphasized.
beats.continuity_physical_rule: Do not alter character designs; pollen remains environmental and from flower contact, not a new permanent accessory.
beats.prohibited_staging: Do not add bears, ocean, storm rain, pier, boat, or any non-script character; do not make Zenny broad or slapstick.
beats.endState: He straightens into a heroic chest-out superhero pose and holds it one blink too long; the smaller bee glides up beside him, perfectly still except her rapid wings, watching, her expression never changing. End on his pose and her flat stare, both at rest, camera locked, as the meadow's gentle hum resumes.

beats.storyBeat: Fuzzby escalates from proud professional to pollen-disaster, sticks his whole face into another flower, emerges wearing a magnificent goatee handlebar moustache of pollen, asks for validation, and Zenny lands the dry answer he did not ask for.
beats.emotionalIntent: The audience should feel the warm snap of a shared joke: Fuzzby wants applause, Zenny gives him truth, and affection leaks through the tease.
beats.want: Fuzzby wants Zenny to certify him as official and impressive; Zenny wants to keep the work orderly without admitting how funny she finds him.
beats.need: Fuzzby needs permission to be ridiculous and still belong; Zenny needs to let her affection crack the deadpan for one tiny second.
beats.crystalTruth: No bear crystal is present; the pollen on Fuzzby's face contradicts his official posture, telling the truth his line cannot: he is not in control, but he is lovable.
beats.kidRead: Fuzzby gets a magnificent goatee handlebar moustache made of pollen and Zenny calls him nuts.
beats.adultRead: The same tease is an affectionate boundary: Zenny sees through the performance and likes him anyway.
beats.theGame: Pollen Run Rules: if Fuzzby can make the mess look official, he wins; Zenny’s rule is that the truth gets the final word.
beats.physicalFeeling: A little tickle under the nose that turns into a held giggle in the throat.
beats.light: Warm amber light catches the pollen dust on Fuzzby's face so the gag reads instantly, while Zenny is lit cleanly and evenly for the deadpan line.
beats.atmosphere: Pollen motes thicken into a soft golden cloud around the flower, building the sneezy-tickly feeling before the reveal.
beats.motionTempo: Fuzzby rockets into the flower too fast, then holds an instant for the moustache reveal — a quick flash, not a dead stop; Zenny's stillness in the same world lets the punchline land.
beats.grade: Honey-gold grade with soft teal shadows; the comedy remains bright and safe with no storm colour yet.

cuts.framing: Drone-style camera chases Fuzzby full-throttle into the next flower, Zenny working calmly in the same frame

beats.storyBeat: Stung by Zenny's joke, Fuzzby wipes at his face and makes the pollen worse, dives into a bigger overcorrection, clips a branch, tumbles into a blossom upside down, and pops back up undimmed with his trademark crash-call as Zenny's eye-roll cracks into a smile.
beats.emotionalIntent: The audience should feel the big comic tumble land as pure carnival fun that refuses to hurt.
beats.want: Fuzzby wants to recover his dignity through an even bigger demonstration of competence.
beats.need: Fuzzby needs to trust that even his mistakes can look heroic if he commits to them fully.
beats.crystalTruth: No bear crystal is present; the pollen keeps thickening on Fuzzby's face even as his confidence does too, telling the truth his crash-call cannot: the mess and the pride are the same thing.
beats.kidRead: Fuzzby smears himself worse, bounces THUMP THUMP THUMP, and pops up shouting his crash-call.
beats.adultRead: Committing fully to a mistake, instantly, is its own kind of bravery — even when it's silly.
beats.theGame: Pollen Run Rules: every flower Fuzzby loses to still counts as a win if he announces it loudly enough.
beats.physicalFeeling: A tumbling carnival drop that refuses to hurt.
beats.light: Golden amber holds steady through the tumble — nothing cools yet; this is still safe, playful chaos.
beats.atmosphere: Pollen clouds and petal bounce build the chaos; branch and blossom obstacles keep the corridor busy.
beats.motionTempo: Full-throttle from frame one: frantic wipe, dive, clip, tumble, pop — Fuzzby never slows until the pop-up; Zenny stays calm and precise in the same frame throughout.
beats.grade: Amber warmth, unbroken — the storm has not touched this beat yet.

One gag arc: setup, impact, recovery, button. Ends here — does not continue into more pollen or the thunder.

beats.storyBeat: Fuzzby hums louder and dives into another flower, popping back up even more covered in pollen than before while Zenny watches in amazement; then a distant rumble of thunder stops him dead mid-hover — the scene's one earned full stop.
beats.emotionalIntent: The audience should feel the escalating comic high suddenly catch in the throat as the thunder lands.
beats.want: Fuzzby wants to keep topping his own chaos until it becomes legendary; Zenny wants to keep enjoying the show without admitting it.
beats.need: Fuzzby needs to notice the world can interrupt him; Zenny needs to shift from amusement to attentiveness the instant it matters.

beats.adultRead: The gag is the last carefree breath before the episode's risk arrives; even silliness has to notice the world changing.

beats.storyBeat: After the distant thunder, Zenny looks toward the sky and reads the shift before Fuzzby does; she names the coming storm, Fuzzby performs confidence under pressure, and immediately gets stuck in another flower as Zenny sighs.
beats.emotionalIntent: The audience should feel the first true prickle of adventure while still getting one last safe laugh: the world is changing, but these two remain themselves.
beats.want: Zenny wants to acknowledge what is coming clearly; Fuzzby wants to prove the storm does not scare him and that pressure is his natural habitat.
beats.need: Zenny needs to trust her read and act on it; Fuzzby needs to let concern become purpose instead of covering it with bravado.
beats.crystalTruth: No bear crystal is present; the light contradiction is the truth: Fuzzby's face says pressure is fine, but the cooling sky and his instant flower-stuck body reveal the wobble underneath.
beats.kidRead: Zenny says a storm is coming, Fuzzby boasts, then immediately flies into a flower and gets stuck.
beats.adultRead: Bravado is often the last little joke before fear arrives; the failure is funny because it will soon become care.
beats.theGame: Pollen Run Rules: when the sky changes, the game becomes ‘who notices first?’ Zenny wins by reading the air; Fuzzby tries to win by pretending pressure is no problem and loses to a flower.

beats.atmosphere: Pollen motes slow and thin; the breeze gains a slightly firmer push through petals, but nothing dangerous is visible yet.
beats.motionTempo: Zenny is slow, precise and watchful; Fuzzby snaps back into full-throttle bravado and immediately over-shoots into the flower.
beats.grade: Amber morning grade gently contaminated by blue-grey in the background, preserving the safe foreground while foreshadowing the storm.

beats.cameraArc: Camera holds still and attentive on Zenny's short sky-glance (punctuation, not coverage), pushes into Fuzzby's bravado, then chases his launch drone-style up into the flower before holding on the stuck-legs-kicking button under Zenny's sigh — the exit.

beats.storyBeat: Aida sits in stillness beside the crystal bowl as the cove quiets around her; the pendant glow carries her into a full-frame inner vision of Keen at the pier with his Mum, checking his satchel again and again while she watches with proud worry.
beats.emotionalIntent: The audience should feel a tender hush: love noticing a child before the child knows he needs it.
beats.want: Aida performs quiet readiness, listening rather than acting too soon.
beats.need: She needs to trust the small signal of love and let it point her toward Keen without controlling his first step.
beats.crystalTruth: Aida’s Rose Quartz pendant brightens softly while her face remains composed; the crystal reveals the pull of care beneath her stillness, steady-warm and gently brightening toward the vision.

beats.adultRead: Aida senses the private ache of a parent letting go and a child trying to look more ready than he feels.

cuts.framing: wide establishing at sanctuary level, 35mm, slow breathing push-in from shoreline toward Aida beside the crystal bowl
cuts.action: Gentle waves roll onto the shore. Aida sits in stillness beside a crystal bowl and runs the wand softly around the rim as a low shimmering tone fills the air; the cove seems to quiet around her.

cuts.framing: hook close-up, 50mm locked-off on Aida and the bowl rim with shallow depth of field
cuts.action: Aida closes her eyes. Her ROSE QUARTZ PENDANT begins to glow, and soft pink light rises across her face.

cuts.framing: Aida POV full-frame vision, 50mm natural lens inside the vision; no sanctuary visible, only soft rose-quartz haze at the outermost margins dissolving into darkness and mist
cuts.action: AIDA’S INNER VISION — THROUGH AIDA’S EYES: A wooden pier appears. A small sailboat waits on bright blue water. Keen stands beside his Mum. He checks the straps on his satchel, then checks them again, then one more time. His Mum watches him with a proud smile that cannot quite hide her worry.

beats.cameraArc: Begin with the cove breathing around Aida, move inward to the honest stillness of her face, then surrender the entire frame to the vision so the audience feels pulled through love rather than shown information.
beats.pauseHold: Hold 1.5 seconds on Keen’s repeated satchel check resolving into his Mum’s proud-but-worried look; the scene goes quiet enough for the parent to feel the goodbye before it happens.

performance.surface: Aida is calm, precise, and open; Keen in the vision is careful and busy; Keen's Mum smiles with pride.
performance.underneath: Aida is being called by care; Keen is trying to make readiness look tidy; Keen's Mum is holding pride and worry in the same smile.
performance.innerThought: Aida: Something tender is moving toward us. Keen: If I check it enough times, I will feel ready. Keen's Mum: I am proud of you, and I wish I could keep you close.

beats.crystalGlow: Aida’s Rose Quartz pendant shifts from steady warm pink to soft brightening glow; the glow contradicts her composed face by showing the urgency of care beneath it.
beats.startState: Aida is seated in stillness beside the crystal bowl at the sanctuary shore, one paw holding the wand at the bowl rim, eyes open and lowered toward the bowl; gentle waves and the empty cove frame her before the vision begins.
beats.keyframePrompt: Polished 3D CGI animation — modern feature-film CGI (Pixar/DreamWorks quality): fully 3D-modelled characters and environments, physically-based rendering, soft global illumination and volumetric lighting, subsurface scattering on plush fur, large expressive eyes with warm catch-lights, realistic materials, cinematic depth of field. NOT 2D, NOT hand-drawn, NOT flat. Use the scene plate for Aida’s sanctuary beside Crystal Cove shore. Aida appears as per the reference image of Aida, seated beside the crystal bowl with the wand resting at the rim in the first frame of the action. CINEMATOGRAPHY: Lens 35mm — natural, present, intimate enough for listening while still holding the cove. Camera movement: very slight organic breathing push — the sanctuary feels alive and attentive. Camera height: seated eye level — we share Aida’s stillness, not looking down on it. Composition: Aida and bowl in the lower third near shore, waves and sheltered foliage creating soft horizontal safety lines, 2-3 subtle ambient crystals low in the background as gentle bokeh accents. Key light: warm rose-gold late-morning light motivated by sun through sheltered canopy and crystal reflections. Fill light: soft cool sea bounce at the edges. Depth of field: moderate-shallow, Aida and bowl sharp, shoreline softly present. Light direction: warm side-front light with a faint pink rim from the crystal bowl area — tenderness beginning to gather. Opening frame only, before the glow intensifies; no pier, no sailboat, no Keen, no Keen's Mum visible in the opening frame.
beats.i2vPrompt: Anchor Image is TRUTH. COPY EXACTLY. Multi-shot sequence with clean cuts between shots. Shot 1 (wide establishing at sanctuary level, 35mm, slow breathing push-in): gentle waves roll onto the shore; Aida sits in stillness beside a crystal bowl and runs the wand softly around the rim; a low shimmering tone fills the air and the cove seems to quiet around her. Shot 2 (cut to hook close-up, 50mm locked-off): Aida closes her eyes; her ROSE QUARTZ PENDANT begins to glow; soft pink light rises across her face, small breath, no dialogue. Shot 3 (cut to Aida POV full-frame vision): the vision bubble fills the entire frame; only soft rose-quartz haze and orb-edge at the outermost margins dissolving into darkness and mist, no sanctuary or Aida visible around it. A wooden pier resolves inside the vision; a small sailboat waits on bright blue water; Keen stands beside his Mum; he checks the straps on his satchel, then checks them again, then one more time; his Mum watches him with a proud smile that cannot quite hide her worry. Keep motion small, readable, and emotionally true; static props hold their positions. Dialogue: none. Style+Audio: 3D CGI Pixar style; Seedance scores gentle wave wash, crystal-bowl rim tone, soft rose resonance, and a barely perceptible distant wind undertone; no spoken voice; Aida’s G note suggested as a low warm harmonic. Duration: 15 seconds. Ratio: 16:9. --no text, watermarks, logos
beats.soundIntent: SFX and timed score begin with soft waves and the crystal bowl’s low shimmering rim tone; ambient cove sound subtly drops as if the world is listening. Aida’s G note enters as a warm rose-quartz harmonic under the vision, unresolved and tender, with a faint distant wind thread arriving only at the edge of the final vision hold.

continuity.opensFrom: Scene 1 ends on distant thunder and a shifted light; this beat opens in late-morning sanctuary calm, carrying only the faint pressure of gathering wind at the edges.
continuity.carryToNext: Aida’s eyes remain closed through the vision glow; the pink light is active and ready to fade back to her face in the next beat.

check.focalSubject: Aida’s listening stillness, then Keen and Keen's Mum inside the vision.
check.emotionalRead: The audience should feel love reaching before danger arrives.
check.heartCheck: The child feels the magic of a secret vision; the parent feels the ache of seeing a child prepare to leave while a mother tries to smile.

beats.writerNote: No dialogue in this beat. The staging protects the theme wordlessly: Aida senses and witnesses rather than explaining.
beats.shot_style: Pixar-quality quiet mystical two-space beat: sanctuary stillness into full-frame vision, no showy camera, emotion led by light and breath.
beats.director_mode: Docter TRUE-heart mode: feeling first, wordless care, beauty held without sentimentality.
beats.audience_feeling_target: A tender inhale: the sense that someone is already loved from far away.
beats.emotional_function: Tiles 1, 3, and 6: wide establish, hook close-up, full-frame vision insert revealing the emotional stakes.

beats.music_emotion: Warm G resonance, rose-quartz tenderness, unresolved because the goodbye has not happened yet.

beats.storyBeat: Back with Aida, the pink glow fades from her eyes. She places a paw over her heart, smiles with quiet knowing, says it is time, then rises and heads purposefully into the forest as the bowl note gives way to distant wind.
beats.emotionalIntent: The audience should feel the tiny shift from listening to loving action: quiet certainty without urgency or panic.
beats.want: Aida wants to answer the call with composed purpose, not alarm.
beats.need: She needs to let her care move her into the world while leaving Keen’s courage to remain his own.
beats.crystalTruth: As Aida’s face softens into a knowing smile, the Rose Quartz glow fades from bright vision-light to steady-warm; the crystal reveals that this is not fear, but love becoming action.
beats.kidRead: Aida saw something important and now knows where to go.
beats.adultRead: A mentor can step toward a child’s danger without stealing the child’s first brave step.
beats.theGame: Follow-the-Note: when the bowl’s note fades into wind, Aida follows where the listening game points.
beats.physicalFeeling: A small steady inhale, like deciding to stand up for someone you love.
beats.light: The pink vision glow releases gently from Aida’s eyes, leaving warm rose-gold key light on her face while cooler wind-shadow gathers at the forest edge.
beats.atmosphere: The still sanctuary air loosens; wave sound returns, the bowl tone thins, and a distant wind begins to thread through leaves.
beats.motionTempo: Slow, graceful, decisive: fade, paw to heart, quiet line, rise, turn, purposeful departure.
beats.grade: Rose-lavender tenderness held over warm gold, with faint cool blue-green in the forest path to foreshadow the storm without breaking safety.

cuts.framing: reaction hold close-up, 50mm locked, eye level on Aida

cuts.voiceTreatment: canonical Aida voice, warm measured softness; spoken as a quiet recognition, not an announcement

cuts.framing: exit wide, 35mm gentle pull-back toward the forest path
cuts.action: She rises gracefully. The bowl’s note fades into the sound of distant wind. Aida turns and heads purposefully into the forest.

beats.cameraArc: Hold still on Aida long enough for the vision to become a decision, then gently widen as she rises so the sanctuary gives her back to the forest path.
beats.pauseHold: Hold 1 second after the paw reaches her heart and before the line; hold another half-beat after “It’s time.” before she rises, so the line lands as a button rather than a transition rush.

performance.surface: Aida is serene, graceful, and purposeful.
performance.underneath: She is moved by what she saw but refuses to turn love into panic.
performance.innerThought: I have seen enough. Now I go meet him where the world is about to get big.

beats.storyBeat: Keen carefully loads and re-checks his supplies while his Mum watches with proud, glassy-eyed love; he performs readiness by insisting he has everything, then betrays himself with one more pocket check.

beats.want: Keen wants to prove he is fully prepared and needs no extra worry from his Mum.
beats.need: Keen needs permission to be loved through the wobble, not just admired for looking capable.
beats.crystalTruth: No crystal yet; the truth is held in the empty space where his Aquamarine will be — his paws perform confidence while his tiny extra check reveals he still wants home to double-check him.
beats.kidRead: Keen keeps checking his adventure things like a very serious little captain.
beats.adultRead: His Mum is proud enough to let him go and scared enough to memorize every movement.
beats.theGame: Ready-Check: Keen plays that if every item is arranged perfectly, leaving will feel perfectly safe; the rule keeps failing because love is not packable.
beats.physicalFeeling: a small chest-tight squeeze of almost-goodbye warmth in the first two seconds
beats.light: Warm golden late-morning sun isolates Keen and the boat in a soft pool, while a faint cool line sits far out on the horizon as the future he is not naming.
beats.atmosphere: Clear breezy air, tiny rope creaks, bright water sparkle, and barely visible unsettled shimmer over the open sea build a tender edge under the safety.
beats.motionTempo: Careful arranging, re-arranging, patting, pausing, then a small fumble-check; Mum stays almost still so his busyness reads as brave performance.
beats.grade: Warm amber and honeyed wood tones preserved with a delicate cool-blue horizon trace.

beats.visibility_rule: Opening frame must not include Squeaky; Squeaky enters only in cut 4.
beats.contact_rule: No contact between boat and dock after push-off; Mum waves from dock.
beats.physics_rule: Boat wobble has buoyant weight; Keen catches himself; supplies stay aboard.
beats.visual_payoff_rule: Mum’s laugh-through-tear is the heart button between gag and surprise.
beats.failed_correction_rule: Do not make the wobble dangerous or scary; it is gentle comic instability.
beats.continuity_physical_rule: Keen wears vacant wristbands throughout; they do not glow.
beats.prohibited_staging: No storm rain, no map loss, no Squeaky before splash.

beats.storyBeat: Squeaky chirps and leaps again; Keen leans over delighted, greets him, nods as if he understands, and the boat glides farther from the dock while Mum grows smaller behind him.
beats.emotionalIntent: The audience feels the bittersweet lift of a new friend arriving exactly as home begins to recede.
beats.want: Keen wants to greet the adventure with delight and keep looking forward.
beats.need: Keen needs to hold both feelings at once: excitement ahead and the ache behind.
beats.crystalTruth: The vacant wristbands remain steady-warm-but-unchanged in the sunlight; they tell the truth that Keen is not leaving love behind, he is carrying it into the new friendship.
beats.kidRead: Keen meets a cheerful dolphin and acts like he understands dolphin chatter.
beats.adultRead: A child can move toward new bonds while the parent becomes smaller in the background; both can be true.
beats.theGame: Dolphin Translator: the rule is that happy chirps count as a full conversation if Keen listens with his whole face; the real rule is connection begins before certainty.
beats.physicalFeeling: a warm lift in the chest as sparkle and chirps replace the goodbye ache in the first two seconds
beats.light: Sparkling gold water light dances around Keen and Squeaky, while Mum on the dock stays warmly visible but gradually softer and smaller.
beats.atmosphere: Bright water droplets, dolphin chirps, gentle breeze and widening sea air create optimism, with the far horizon retaining a thin cool unsettled line.
beats.motionTempo: Buoyant leap, delighted lean, playful nod, then slow widening drift into mixed nervous-excited stillness.
beats.grade: Golden-blue adventure glow with a faint storm-cool thread on the distant horizon; warm foreground does not erase the ache behind.

cuts.framing: wide boat-and-water shot, 24mm, buoyant tilt following Squeaky
cuts.action: Squeaky chirps happily and leaps again through the sparkling water; Keen leans over the side, delighted.
cuts.delivery: Delighted, open, a little breathless from the surprise.

cuts.framing: medium two-shot from boat side, 35mm, gentle bobbing camera
cuts.action: Squeaky chatters; Keen nods as though he fully understands.
cuts.dialogue: KEEN: Nice to meet you too.
cuts.delivery: Matter-of-fact wonder, as if dolphin conversation is completely reasonable.

cuts.framing: longer wide from behind the boat, 35mm slow dolly-out
cuts.action: The boat glides farther from the dock; Keen's Mum grows smaller behind him.

cuts.framing: close on Keen turning forward, 50mm, slow settling hold
cuts.action: Keen looks ahead, a little nervous, a little excited, a lot of both.

beats.cameraArc: Start buoyant and close to the new friendship, then slowly widen until the dock becomes memory behind Keen, ending on his mixed face looking forward.
beats.pauseHold: Hold 2 seconds on Keen’s final face: nervous and excited together, with the vacant wristbands just present in frame.

performance.surface: Keen is delighted by Squeaky and amused by the chatter.
performance.underneath: He is already missing home while discovering the world might welcome him too.
performance.innerThought: Keen: I’m really going. Also… I just made a dolphin friend. Maybe I can be both scared and happy.

beats.crystalGlow: Vacant wristbands catch warm sunlight but do not glow; their steady presence carries Mum’s promise into the open water.
beats.startState: Keen is in the sailboat moving away from the dock wearing vacant wristbands; Squeaky is beside the boat in the water after his first splash; Keen's Mum is still visible on the dock behind him.

continuity.opensFrom: Squeaky has just risen beside the boat and Keen has reacted with wonder; Mum remains on the dock as the boat moves away.
continuity.carryToNext: Keen leaves the pier heading into open water with Squeaky beside him, wearing vacant wristbands; Mum is now smaller behind him.

check.focalSubject: Keen between new friend and receding Mum
check.emotionalRead: The adventure is beginning, and it contains both joy and ache.
check.heartCheck: The child feels the thrill of a dolphin friend; the parent feels the dock getting smaller behind a child who is already growing into the world.

beats.storyBeat: Keen sails on endless open water, performing confidence as Squeaky keeps pace beside the boat; their first playful exchange names the dolphin and briefly makes the sea feel friendly.
beats.emotionalIntent: Let the audience feel the wide-open thrill of independence with a tiny wobble underneath: Keen is delighted, but the ocean is already bigger than his certainty.
beats.want: Keen wants to sound ready, friendly, and completely in charge of his own adventure.
beats.need: Keen needs to admit that being new out here means he may need companionship and guidance, not just confidence.
beats.crystalTruth: Keen has no Aquamarine crystal yet; the vacant wristbands stay quiet and unlit, contradicting his bright performance by quietly holding the fact that courage has not been named or tested yet.
beats.kidRead: Keen makes a new squeaky dolphin friend and gives him the perfect name.
beats.adultRead: A child leaving home often performs ease by becoming extra chatty; connection is already becoming his real safety line.
beats.theGame: Name-the-sound game: if a friend sounds like their name, the world feels briefly understandable.
beats.physicalFeeling: A small buoyant lift in the chest, like the first exciting push away from shore before you notice how much water is around you.
beats.light: Clear blue sea light still carries late-morning warmth on the sail and Keen’s face, but the far horizon is a little cooler than the foreground.
beats.atmosphere: Open air, gentle spray, and soft glints on the water keep the beat spacious; the emptiness around the boat is felt but not yet threatening.
beats.motionTempo: Easy bobbing, small boat sway, Squeaky’s playful pops timed between Keen’s lines; confident rhythm with a slight over-careful edge.
beats.grade: Bright aquamarine-blue with warm highlights, subtly desaturating toward the horizon to preserve the first hint of uncertainty.

cuts.framing: wide establishing, 24mm, low boat-height side angle, slight organic breathing push with the boat travelling screen-right
cuts.action: Open water stretches endlessly around the small sailboat. Keen stands in the boat trying to sail confidently while Squeaky keeps pace beside the boat, popping up every so often.
cuts.dialogue: KEEN: Hi! I’m Keen! Nice to meet you, Squeaky!
cuts.delivery: bright, breathless friendliness; a little too eager to prove this is all going perfectly.

cuts.framing: medium two-shot from the boat rail, 35mm, gentle handheld bob synced to the water
cuts.action: Squeaky chatters happily beside the boat. Keen nods like that settles it.

cuts.framing: medium close on Keen with Squeaky rising frame-right in soft background, 50mm, tiny push-in that lands on Keen’s smile
cuts.action: Keen accepts the dolphin’s chatter as a proper introduction while the boat continues forward across the open water.
cuts.dialogue: KEEN: Squeaky. Perfect. You really do sound squeaky.
cuts.delivery: pleased with his own logic; warm, amused, and trying to sound grown-up.

cuts.framing: wide exit two-shot, 24mm, slight lift as Squeaky leaps high beside the boat
cuts.action: Squeaky leaps high beside the boat. Keen laughs, the sea sparkling around them for one last safe-feeling beat.

beats.cameraArc: Start wide and airy to sell independence, settle into a friendly two-shot for connection, then end wide again so the boat’s smallness quietly returns under the laugh.
beats.pauseHold: A half-second hold after Keen’s nod before his second line, letting the child register that he believes he understood Squeaky perfectly.

performance.surface: Keen is cheerful, capable, and socially brave with his new ocean friend.
performance.underneath: He is filling the size of the sea with chatter so he does not have to feel how far he is from the pier.
performance.innerThought: If I make this feel like an adventure, maybe it won’t feel like leaving.

check.focalSubject: Keen performing cheerful readiness in the small sailboat.
check.emotionalRead: Exciting independence with a tiny hidden wobble underneath.
check.heartCheck: The child feels the fun of meeting Squeaky; the parent feels the little ache of a child talking bravely because the world suddenly got big.

beats.storyBeat: The playful certainty thins as Keen checks the map, looks ahead, checks again, and discovers that his confident navigation is mostly performance.
beats.emotionalIntent: Make the audience feel the first stomach-flutter of not knowing, while still letting the line land as a gentle kid-friendly joke.
beats.want: Keen wants the map to prove he knows exactly where he is going.
beats.need: Keen needs to tolerate uncertainty without pretending it is certainty.
beats.crystalTruth: Keen’s vacant wristbands remain unlit and still; his face performs cleverness, but the absence of glow tells the truth that he is not grounded yet.
beats.kidRead: Keen turns the map upside down and decides he is going one of the directions.
beats.adultRead: This is the moment a child’s rehearsed confidence starts to fail, but he is still trying to make the wobble funny.
beats.theGame: Which-way-is-north game: the rule is that turning the map should make the world make sense, but the joke reveals it does not.
beats.physicalFeeling: A light flutter in the tummy, like realizing you might not know the way but hoping nobody noticed.
beats.light: Warm highlights on the boat begin to flatten; a cooler blue-silver fill creeps across the map and Keen’s paws, isolating the uncertainty.
beats.atmosphere: The air is still mostly clear, but the horizon looks wider and less friendly; the water texture grows slightly choppier under the boat.
beats.motionTempo: Small, precise, over-careful: look down, look ahead, look down again, rotate map, hold the joke.
beats.grade: Blue-aquamarine with warmth draining from the midtones, preserving the feeling that safety is beginning to slip.

cuts.framing: context mid, 35mm, over-the-map angle from inside the boat, tiny handheld sway
cuts.action: Keen laughs from Squeaky’s leap, then looks at his map. Then ahead. Then back at his map. A little uncertain.
cuts.dialogue: KEEN: Okay… so if this is north… then I am definitely going…
cuts.delivery: slowing as confidence runs out; trying to keep the sentence alive long enough for the answer to appear.

cuts.framing: insert close-up, 85mm, locked on the loose map in Keen’s paws with sea blur behind
cuts.action: Keen turns the map upside down.

cuts.framing: hook close-up on Keen, 50mm, locked for the comic honesty
cuts.action: Keen looks from the upside-down map to the open water, realizing the map has not solved anything.
cuts.delivery: tiny proud recovery; a child trying to make uncertainty sound official.

cuts.framing: wide two-shot, 24mm, slow pull-back to reveal more empty water around the boat
cuts.action: Squeaky stays beside the boat as Keen holds the map and the sailboat continues across the increasingly open sea.

beats.cameraArc: Move from practical map-reading closeness to an honest locked close-up, then pull back so the joke leaves behind the real scale of the water.
beats.pauseHold: Hold 10 frames on the upside-down map before Keen’s button line, then a half-second on his face after the line.

performance.surface: Keen is making a joke and pretending navigation is under control.
performance.underneath: He has just felt the first real crack in his readiness.
performance.innerThought: If I can make this sound funny, it won’t count as being lost.

check.emotionalRead: Funny uncertainty turning into a real flutter of not knowing.
check.heartCheck: The child laughs at the upside-down map; the parent recognizes the fragile moment when a child’s plan stops being enough.

beats.shot_style: Natural 35mm-to-50mm performance comedy with a widening exit for emotional scale.

beats.storyBeat: The storm arrives fast: a gust slams the sail, the boat lurches, Squeaky disappears beneath the water, rain becomes sheets, and the wind snatches Keen’s map away, leaving him frozen and lost.
beats.emotionalIntent: Deliver the stomach-drop: the world turns too big, too loud, too fast, and Keen’s performance of readiness fails in his hands.
beats.want: Keen wants to keep control of the boat, the map, and his brave self-image all at once.
beats.need: Keen needs to feel the fear clearly instead of out-talking it, because real courage cannot begin until the performance breaks.
beats.crystalTruth: The vacant wristbands stay visible but unlit as rain hits them; they do not rescue him, forcing the truth that he is scared and alone with the next choice.
beats.kidRead: The wind steals the map and it vanishes into the storm.
beats.adultRead: This is the child’s first irreversible consequence away from home: the plan is gone, and no parent can immediately hand it back.
beats.theGame: Keep-the-map game: the rule is simple — don’t let go — and the storm changes the rules faster than Keen can keep up.
beats.physicalFeeling: A sudden drop in the stomach, like the floor tilting under your feet.
beats.light: Warmth drains abruptly; blue-grey storm light swallows the boat, with brief cold highlights on wet wood, sail, and Keen’s empty wristbands.
beats.atmosphere: Wind streaks, rain escalation, spray, and a closing grey horizon compress the air until open water becomes a wall.
beats.motionTempo: Abrupt lurch, frantic grab, accelerating rain, then a hard freeze after the map disappears.
beats.grade: Cold blue-grey with desaturated storm contrast, preserving the sense that the world has become too big and unreadable.

cuts.framing: action mid, 24mm, low canted boat-height with a sharp organic jolt but no dutch exaggeration
cuts.action: A gust of wind hits. The sail flaps hard. The boat lurches. Keen stumbles sideways and grabs the mast.
cuts.delivery: startled and unguarded; the first sound not performed for anyone.

cuts.framing: wide storm build, 24mm, rapid push with rain beginning to streak the lens plane
cuts.action: The sky darkens. Clouds roll in faster than Keen expected. Squeaky disappears beneath the water. Another gust hits stronger. Rain starts as a few drops, then many, then sheets. The sea roughens and the boat creaks as Keen tries to keep hold of the map while steering. The wind snatches it from his paws.
cuts.delivery: urgent, breath catching on the final dash as the map leaves his reach.

cuts.framing: climax shot, 35mm tracking from Keen’s lunge to the map whipping away into grey rain
cuts.action: Keen lunges for the map and misses. The map whips through the air and vanishes into the storm.

cuts.framing: reaction hold close-up, 50mm locked on Keen in rain with the horizon erased behind him
cuts.action: Keen stands frozen. Rainwater sloshes around his feet. The horizon disappears behind sheets of grey.
cuts.delivery: under his breath; small at first, then the last words break through because the truth has arrived.

beats.cameraArc: Begin with the camera jolting with the boat, chase the lost map just enough to feel the miss, then lock into a 50mm hold so the loss lands on Keen’s face rather than spectacle.
beats.pauseHold: Hold on Keen frozen in the rain for a full 1.5 seconds after the map vanishes before and through the lost line.

performance.surface: Keen fights to keep the boat and map under control.
performance.underneath: The brave mask drops; his body understands he is lost before he can say it.
performance.innerThought: Mum isn’t here. The map is gone. I don’t know what to do.

beats.emotionalIntent: The audience should feel a comic buzz abruptly caught in the throat: the funny bee suddenly understands the danger before he can make it funny.

beats.adultRead: The parent sees the first small moral turn: a clownish character becomes serious because empathy arrives faster than pride.
beats.theGame: Fuzzby’s private Storm Situation Drill: if he can keep talking, he can pretend the storm is just another silly obstacle — until the tiny boat breaks the game’s rule.

beats.motionTempo: Fast, jittery flight lands on a hard freeze; Fuzzby’s body stops dead while his wings continue beating rapidly and continuously, with only the tiny paw slowly lifting.

beats.storyBeat: The crystal beacon reaches Keen through the storm, hope lifts his face, then Squeaky is dragged under by the drift net and Keen chooses to dive without waiting to feel ready.
beats.emotionalIntent: A sudden lift of hope becomes a stomach-drop of fear, then a brave choice made through that fear.
beats.want: Keen wants to look capable and stay in control of the boat.
beats.need: Keen needs to let fear be present and still choose care over self-protection.
beats.crystalTruth: Keen’s face tries to snap into action, but his vacant wristbands stay dark and heavy at first, revealing that courage has not become certainty; the need is the choice through fear, not the absence of fear.
beats.kidRead: The light is magic, Squeaky is stuck, and Keen does the big brave splash.
beats.adultRead: A child stops performing readiness and makes a real risk-based decision because someone else needs him.
beats.theGame: Storm Rescue: the rule is simple and child-clear — when the friend dips under, the next right move matters more than looking ready.

beats.pillar: Ripple — earned belonging after the storm
beats.storyBeat: The lifted group walks together along the golden-hour beach. Fuzzby buzzes up beside Keen with sudden solemn importance and performs a tiny friendship interview about honey; Keen, still breathless from everything, answers honestly, and Fuzzby receives it like destiny.
beats.emotionalIntent: The audience feels the relief of ordinary silliness returning after danger: Keen is not being tested for heroism now, just welcomed into a ridiculous little ritual.
beats.want: Fuzzby wants to make the new hero instantly fit into his world by proving they are honey-compatible; Keen wants to answer without making a big deal of himself.
beats.need: Fuzzby needs to turn awe into friendship instead of worship; Keen needs to learn that belonging can be small, funny, and unearned by bravery.
beats.crystalTruth: Keen’s face plays amused and casual, but his Aquamarine wristbands hold a steady-warm-but-changed glow, revealing he is still absorbing the rescue and the welcome beneath the laugh; the other bears’ crystals remain soft and steady, holding him without pressure.
beats.kidRead: Fuzzby is asking the most serious silly question in the world: do you like honey?
beats.adultRead: After a frightening rite of passage, the first safe question is not about courage or trauma — it is about snacks, and that is how a child is allowed to come back to ordinary life.
beats.theGame: The Honey Compatibility Test: Fuzzby’s made-up rule is that one honest answer about honey can decide whether a friendship is meant to be.
beats.physicalFeeling: A warm little bubble rising in the chest — the body realises the danger has passed before the mind has caught up.
beats.light: Warm golden-hour side light isolates Keen and Fuzzby from the walking group, with soft aquamarine glints on wet sand and Keen’s crystal-set wristbands catching one small sparkle on the honey answer.
beats.atmosphere: Clear after-storm air, gentle surf, damp sand sheen, sparkling leaf droplets and a few drifting pollen-like motes from the beach grasses; the world breathes out after the storm.
beats.motionTempo: Easy walking base rhythm, then Fuzzby’s sudden fast, hovering seriousness creates comic syncopation; Keen’s replies are slower and still a little winded.
beats.grade: Warm amber-gold with storm-washed aquamarine shadows; saturated but gentle, preserving the memory of rain in the highlights.

FUZZBY: "How do you feel about honey?" Shot 4 (cut to honest 50mm close-up on Keen, locked): Keen looks at him, breath still settling, and answers simply. KEEN: "Uh… I like it?" Shot 5 (cut to Fuzzby reaction close, quick delighted push-in then held take): Beat. beats.want: Fuzzby wants to claim Keen immediately as the ideal honey friend and make the friendship huge; Zenny wants to keep the welcome from overwhelming Keen.
beats.need: Fuzzby needs permission to love big without swallowing the other person; Zenny needs to show care by setting a tiny boundary, not by shutting the feeling down.

beats.kidRead: Fuzzby lists every kind of honey thing like a runaway snack machine, then gets gently reeled in by Zenny.
beats.adultRead: A new community welcomes Keen with delight, but Zenny’s boundary keeps that affection safe and breathable; love is warmest when it leaves room.
beats.theGame: The Honey Friendship Treaty: Fuzzby invents endless honey clauses, while Zenny’s rule is that friendship only works one sweet thing at a time.

beats.grade: Warm amber-gold comedy with clean aquamarine after-storm accents; highlights stay soft so the gag feels affectionate, not frantic in a scary way.
